title: hieracium valdepilosum subsp. valdepilosum
intro: Hieracium valdepilosum subsp. valdepilosum, also known as the hairy hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. The hairy hawkweed is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestion. In traditional herbal medicine, the hairy hawkweed is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to utilize its medicinal properties.
